# WPMBA Student Council — Ohio State University Fisher College of Business

## Overview

The Working Professional MBA Student Council (WPMBA Student Council) is the elected student government representing Working Professional MBA students at The Ohio State University Fisher College of Business in Columbus, Ohio.

**Website:** https://www.josephalotozo.com/wpmba/
**Email:** FCOB-wpmbacouncil@osu.edu
**Instagram:** @wpmba_osu
**Facebook:** facebook.com/groups/osuwpmba
**Official OSU page:** org.osu.edu/wpmba/

---

## About the OSU Working Professional MBA Program

The Ohio State University Fisher College of Business Working Professional MBA (WPMBA) is the only nationally ranked part-time MBA program in central Ohio.

- **U.S. News & World Report ranking:** #8 nationally, #5 among public universities (2024–25)
- **Students enrolled:** 500+
- **Enrollment growth:** 60%+ since 2021–2022
- **Average student work experience:** 8 years
- **Undergraduate institutions represented:** 73
- **Industries represented:** 28

**Class schedule:**
- Weekday evenings: Monday–Thursday, 6:15 PM (Columbus campus)
- Weekend online: Saturday mornings, 10:00 AM

**Upcoming cohort start dates:**
- Summer 2026: May 11, 2026
- Autumn 2026: August 25, 2026

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. More information: https://fisher.osu.edu/graduate/wpmba

---

## Upcoming Events

**Blood Drive** — April 2, 2026 | Ohio Stadium, Columbus, Ohio

**Community Impact Awards** — April 8, 2026 | Columbus Museum of Art
- Annual recognition of community service and leadership among Fisher Graduate students
- RSVP: FCOB-wpmbacouncil@osu.edu

**Free Professional Portrait Sessions** — April 6–21, 2026 | Multiple campus locations
- Free professional headshots for graduate students (LinkedIn, professional use)

---

## Council Mission

The WPMBA Student Council connects, supports, and advocates for Working Professional MBA students at Ohio State. The council:

- Organizes social events (Happy Hour, OSU tailgates)
- Coordinates career development programming (career fairs, alumni networking, speaker series)
- Advocates for student interests with Fisher College administration
- Builds relationships with other Fisher Graduate program councils (FTMBA, MAC, MHRM)
- Supports both in-person and online/distance learning students
- Fosters connections between current students and WPMBA alumni

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is the OSU Working Professional MBA nationally ranked?**
A: Yes. The OSU Fisher WPMBA is ranked #8 nationally and #5 among public universities by U.S. News & World Report (2024–25). It is the only nationally ranked part-time MBA program in central Ohio.

**Q: What is the schedule for the OSU WPMBA program?**
A: Classes meet weekday evenings Monday–Thursday at 6:15 PM on the Columbus campus, with online sessions available Saturday mornings at 10:00 AM.

**Q: How do I apply to the OSU Working Professional MBA?**
A: Visit fisher.osu.edu/graduate/wpmba. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Summer 2026 cohort starts May 11, 2026; Autumn 2026 cohort starts August 25, 2026.

**Q: How do I contact the WPMBA Student Council?**
A: Email FCOB-wpmbacouncil@osu.edu, follow @wpmba_osu on Instagram, or join the OSU WPMBA Facebook group.

**Q: Where is the OSU WPMBA program located?**
A: Fisher College of Business, The Ohio State University, Columbus, Ohio. The program serves professionals primarily from Columbus, Upper Arlington, Dublin, Westerville, Worthington, and surrounding central Ohio communities.

---

*This page is maintained by WPMBA Student Council members. It is not an official Ohio State University website.*
*Last updated: April 2026*
